name: Focaccia (Italian Flat Bread)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine 1 1/2 cups flour, yeast, 1 tablespoon sugar, and 1 teaspoon salt. Mix well.
2. Gradually add water and oil to the flour mixture. Blend at low speed until moistened.
3. Beat on medium speed for 3 minutes. Stir in enough remaining flour by hand to make a firm dough.
4. Knead on a floured surface for 5 to 8 minutes, adding flour as needed. Place in a greased bowl and turn to grease the top. Cover and let rise in a warm place about 40 minutes (20 minutes for Quick Rise yeast).
5. Prepare onion topping: In a small skillet, sauté onion in butter until soft. Stir in 1/3 teaspoon sugar and 1/8 teaspoon salt.
6. Punch down dough. On a lightly floured surface, shape dough into a ball and place on a greased cookie sheet.
7. Flatten to a 10-inch circle. Cut a circle about 1 inch from the edge, cutting almost through to the cookie sheet. Prick the center with a fork.
8. Spread onion topping over the pricked dough. Cover and let rise in a warm place about 30 minutes (15 minutes for Quick Rise).
9. Bake at 375°F for 25 to 30 minutes until golden brown.
Variation: Omit onion topping. Instead of pricking the top, cut a criss-cross design with a sharp knife. Brush bread with an egg glaze made by combining 1 egg yolk and 1 tablespoon milk or water. Sprinkle with sesame seeds if desired.
